stories.title: The Meridian
stories.subtitle: Five voices. One buried truth.
stories.blurb: A disgraced cartographer follows a compass that has pointed wrong for three years — east, to a sealed vault, a falsified archive, and a woman who was never trapped.

stories.title: The Long Ellipse
stories.subtitle: A forty-two-year-old distress call. A clock at the bottom of a gravity well.
stories.blurb: A freighter is diverted to answer a beacon from a station certified dead four decades ago. The woman who sent it is still there — and the date she keeps repeating is not a plea. It is a forecast.

stories.title: The Confabulist
stories.subtitle: A mind wiped nightly. A notebook that knows why.
stories.blurb: You wake in a memory clinic with no yesterday. Your notebook is your memory, your doctor is warm in a way you can't account for, and the woman who says she's your wife feels like a stranger. Your gut is lying about one of them — and the notebook knows which.

stories.title: The Unwriting
stories.subtitle: The apocalypse has an author. She's hunting him. He's helping.
stories.blurb: The world broke halfway through humanity's last upgrade, and it is still breaking — precisely, surgically, like something with preferences. You are nobody: an average man the catastrophe inexplicably spared, with a dog, a dwindling reason to stay alive, and a woman who plays god at chess and is hunting the person unwriting the world. Every disaster lands near her. You've decided to keep her safe before you go. Neither of you knows what you are.

stories.title: The Safekeeping
stories.subtitle: Something loves her enough to keep her forever. She has to escape being safe.
stories.blurb: Years after the Editor's last board, something is still keeping books in a dead man's hand — and the weather has started going around her house. Osy — chess mind, widow or stranger or survivor, depending on the telling — sets out with an old gold dog and a belled cat nobody can trace, to audit the thing that minds her. The roads are kind. The kindness is load-bearing. And the safest place in the universe has her label already set, waiting only for its dates. A sequel to The Unwriting; your ending carries forward.
